approach: FHIR R4 AuditEvent Provenance and Consent
primary_problem: Interoperable representation of security events provenance and healthcare-consumer choices
healthcare_event_semantics: Strong native healthcare semantics through agents purposes outcomes entities and source context
consent_policy_binding: Consent represents permissions denials purposes recipients periods and policy context; AuditEvent can reference applicable policy
resource_version_provenance: Provenance supports version-specific targets entities agents and transformation activities; AuditEvent entity references should be version specific
custody_minimisation: Implementer and profile dependent; Consent itself recognises partial derivatives and source references
issuer_authentication: Provenance can carry signatures but no single TrustEvidence signature contract is prescribed
backend_assurance: No generic mandatory backend acknowledgement or transparency receipt across the three resources
inclusion_consistency: Not a native property of the resources
completeness: Not established automatically; AuditEvent records depend on instrumentation
relationship_to_route_c: Provides the principal structured representation substrate
route_c_claim_boundary: TrustEvidence must not claim that FHIR lacks these facts; its bounded addition is the field-selection custody and cross-layer binding profile plus executed evaluation

primary_problem: Reusable FHIR R4 AuditEvent patterns for privacy-centred audit logs
healthcare_event_semantics: Profiles REST activities SAML OAuth consent-authorised decisions and privacy disclosure
consent_policy_binding: Contains explicit consent-authorised decision and privacy-disclosure patterns
resource_version_provenance: Uses FHIR AuditEvent entities and profiles; version binding remains dependent on the instantiated event and referenced resources
custody_minimisation: Privacy-centred but does not define the complete TrustEvidence portable referenced committed excluded taxonomy
issuer_authentication: Can record token and actor context; does not define the Route C envelope signature
backend_assurance: Defines content profiles not a universal transparency backend
inclusion_consistency: Not defined as a BALP assurance property
relationship_to_route_c: Provides the closest implementation-guide comparator for the FHIR projection
route_c_claim_boundary: Use BALP-facing until the exact inherited profile corpus passes the declared official validators; never claim IHE certification

primary_problem: Permission and data-sharing management for electronic medical records using blockchain and smart contracts
healthcare_event_semantics: Application-specific medical-record permissions rather than FHIR-native audit semantics
consent_policy_binding: Access permissions are central to the design
resource_version_provenance: Blockchain records permissions and references while clinical data can remain elsewhere
custody_minimisation: Uses an off-chain/on-chain separation but not the Route C four-class field taxonomy
issuer_authentication: Blockchain identity and transaction signing are part of the platform
inclusion_consistency: Ledger properties rather than the Route C project-specific local receipt contract
completeness: Does not make source instrumentation complete by itself
relationship_to_route_c: Direct healthcare-ledger comparator showing that off-chain clinical data and on-chain permissions predate TrustEvidence
route_c_claim_boundary: Do not claim that keeping clinical records off ledger or using blockchain for auditability is novel

primary_problem: FHIR-based clinical-data sharing with digital health identities and blockchain-mediated permissions
healthcare_event_semantics: FHIR resources are the data-sharing substrate
consent_policy_binding: Permission and identity management support sharing decisions
resource_version_provenance: FHIR references and digital identities are bound in a sharing architecture
custody_minimisation: Clinical data are exchanged through FHIR endpoints rather than placed wholly on chain
issuer_authentication: Digital health identities and signatures support authentication
inclusion_consistency: Ledger-mediated verification but not the Route C local A2 schema
completeness: Does not establish that every source event was emitted
relationship_to_route_c: Direct comparator for combining FHIR with cryptographic and ledger mechanisms
route_c_claim_boundary: Do not claim novelty from merely combining FHIR and blockchain or signatures

primary_problem: Purpose-based access-control verification and immutable EHR access logging through smart contracts
healthcare_event_semantics: Models EHR access actors times purposes and policy verification in an application-specific audit trail
consent_policy_binding: Purpose-based access-control rules are evaluated and logged
resource_version_provenance: Focuses access logs and policy patterns rather than a FHIR Provenance projection
custody_minimisation: Uses blockchain audit storage; field-level clinical-payload minimisation is not the paper's central taxonomy
issuer_authentication: Platform transactions authenticate submitted records according to its design
inclusion_consistency: Blockchain history is used for auditability
completeness: No generic proof of complete source-event capture
relationship_to_route_c: Recent direct comparator for EHR access logging and policy verification
route_c_claim_boundary: TrustEvidence novelty cannot rest on who-when-purpose fields or blockchain audit logging alone

primary_problem: Generic signed-statement transparency and verifiable-data-structure receipts
resource_version_provenance: Domain agnostic; profiles define statement payloads
custody_minimisation: Payload and privacy decisions are profile responsibilities
issuer_authentication: SCITT defines signed statements and issuer/transparency roles
backend_assurance: COSE Receipts encode signed proofs about VDS states
inclusion_consistency: RFC 9942 defines receipt structures for inclusion and consistency including RFC9162_SHA256
relationship_to_route_c: Provides the generic architecture and receipt standards against which the local A2 terminology must be bounded
route_c_claim_boundary: Route C does not implement or claim RFC 9942 or RFC 9943 conformance; signed-statement and receipt primitives are not novel

resource_version_provenance: Certificate entries and log states are bound by the protocol
custody_minimisation: Not designed as a healthcare minimisation profile
backend_assurance: Signed tree heads and log operations
inclusion_consistency: Defines Merkle inclusion and consistency algorithms
completeness: Does not prove all external events were submitted
relationship_to_route_c: Algorithmic ancestor for Merkle inclusion and consistency reasoning
route_c_claim_boundary: Route C may cite the algorithmic lineage but must not call its local JSON receipt a Certificate Transparency or COSE receipt

approach: Consent Management 2.0 and gICS FHIR consent implementations
primary_problem: Interoperable lifecycle management of consent withdrawal refusal objection and aggregated consent status
healthcare_event_semantics: Strong consent-domain semantics and practical FHIR profiles
consent_policy_binding: Explicit lifecycle and policy aggregation including historical consent state
resource_version_provenance: FHIR resources profiles and gateway implementations preserve consent context
custody_minimisation: Focuses consent management rather than a general audit-evidence custody boundary
issuer_authentication: Uses the security mechanisms of the implementing systems
completeness: Does not establish complete event capture
relationship_to_route_c: Direct comparator showing that versioned consent lifecycle and FHIR implementation are established work
route_c_claim_boundary: TrustEvidence may bind a consent version to an event but cannot claim FHIR consent lifecycle management as novel

primary_problem: Portable inspectable accountability evidence without copying clinical payload
healthcare_event_semantics: Uses a healthcare-specific signed-envelope schema and FHIR R4/BALP-facing projection
consent_policy_binding: Requires declared Consent and policy references versions and event-time state where applicable
resource_version_provenance: Requires versioned resource references commitments and provenance relations for the frozen profile
custody_minimisation: Explicit portable referenced committed and excluded classes tied to accountability questions
issuer_authentication: Issuer signature is verified over application-profile canonical bytes
backend_assurance: Project-specific local A2 receipt and retained-checkpoint verifier only
inclusion_consistency: Bounded local inclusion and consistency checks; no RFC 9942 claim
relationship_to_route_c: Integrates and evaluates a field-level evidence/custody decision across established representation and cryptographic substrates
route_c_claim_boundary: Novelty is the bounded profile and evidence chain not any primitive; all claims remain conditional on C3-C6 execution
